top_categories.workout_context: 8,200ft of climbing means repeated surges above threshold. VO2max work is how you survive the fifth climb, not just the first.
Race-pace efforts that mimic The Majestics' demands. Pacing, fueling, and tactical decisions under fatigue. Practice the race before July.
The Majestics' mixed terrain forces constant power changes. Surge over the rough stuff, settle on the smooth, repeat for 66 miles.
66 miles on mixed terrain burns energy faster than the distance suggests. Train the fade resistance now.
At The Majestics, surges push you above threshold and you have to recover while still riding. Over-unders train exactly that.
124ft/mi means long efforts at or near FTP on every climb. If your threshold cracks at minute 15, you'll walk the rest.
8,200ft of climbing on grades that change every minute. You need to switch from seated to standing without losing rhythm.
How long can you hold 105% FTP before you blow? At The Majestics, that number decides whether you bridge or get dropped.
88–92% FTP is where you'll spend the hardest sustained miles of The Majestics. Train it until it feels boring.
Short, max efforts are unavoidable. Punchy climbs, gap closures, attacks on mixed terrain. If you haven't trained them, you'll crack.
Base fitness for The Majestics. Without this, the hard workouts break you down instead of building you up.
Low-cadence force work for 8,200ft of climbing. The slow, grinding ascents where you can't spin—you push.

race_overlay.heat: July in Aigle / Villars-sur-Ollon, Switzerland can bring heat. Complete 3–4 heat exposure sessions in the final 2 weeks before The Majestics. Increase sodium intake 48 hours before race day.
race_overlay.nutrition: Target 40–60g carbs/hour for The Majestics’s 66 miles. Front-load calories in the first half. One bottle per hour minimum, more in heat.
race_overlay.altitude: Moderate altitude at The Majestics. Arrive 2–3 days early. Reduce intensity expectations by 5–10%. Hydrate aggressively.
race_overlay.terrain: Mixed terrain at The Majestics requires surface adaptability. Include weekly rides on mixed terrain to build confidence.

pack_summary: This 10-workout pack focuses on VO2max, Race Simulation, and Gravel Specific to prepare you for 66 miles of mixed terrain in Aigle / Villars-sur-Ollon, Switzerland.
